2606.17904.title: DiagFlowBench: Evaluating How Language Models Handle Off-Procedure Inputs in Grounded Diagnostic Dialogue
2606.17904.paper_summary: Language models increasingly serve as advisory systems in maintenance operations. To prevent hallucination, recent systems ground these models in procedural documentation to constrain them to approved steps. In practice, however, operator queries frequently stray from this path, requiring models to recognise out-of-scope inputs mid-conversation, a dynamic that current benchmarks rarely prioritise. We introduce DiagFlowBench, a dataset of 50 industrial diagnostic flowcharts from a consumer manufacturer converted into 1,676 multi-turn conversations that contrast compliant with out-of-scope utterances. Evaluating a panel of ten commercial and open-weight models reveals high variability in abstention rates, with models commonly selecting a real but contextually inadequate step rather than fabricating facts. The inherent plausibility and authority of this mapped but wrong advice exposes a challenging vulnerability for grounding systems.
2606.17904.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型越來越多地作為維護操作中的諮詢系統。為了防止幻覺，最近的系統將這些模型基於程序文檔，以限制它們遵循已批准的步驟。然而，在實踐中，操作員的查詢經常偏離這條路徑，要求模型在對話過程中識別超出範疇的輸入，這種動態在當前基準中很少被優先考慮。我們介紹了DiagFlowBench，一個包含50個來自消費品製造商的工業診斷流程圖的數據集，轉換為1,676個多輪對話，對比合規與超出範疇的發言。對十個商業和開放權重模型的評估顯示，棄權率存在高度變異，模型通常選擇一個真實但在上下文中不充分的步驟，而不是虛構事實。這種映射但錯誤建議的固有可信性和權威性暴露了基礎系統的一個挑戰性脆弱性。
2606.17904.author: Guillermo Gil de Avalle et.al.
2606.17904.authors: Guillermo Gil de Avalle, Laura Maruster, Shaina Raza, Christos Emmanouilidis
